name: Sourdough San Francisco Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large warmed bowl, sprinkle the yeast over the water and stir to dissolve. Let stand until bubbly.
2. Blend in the sourdough starter, sugar, salt, and vinegar.
3. Gradually beat in 3 cups of the flour. Beat for at least 3 minutes.
4. Turn the batter into a large oiled glass or ceramic bowl and cover with a towel. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk (about 1 hour).
5. Combine 1 cup of the remaining flour with baking soda. Stir the batter, then add the flour-baking soda mixture.
6. Gradually add the remaining flour to make a stiff dough. Turn out onto a floured board and knead, adding additional flour only as needed to prevent sticking (about 300 strokes of folding and turning or until smooth and elastic).
7. Sprinkle a greased baking sheet with cornmeal. Form the dough into 2 oblong loaves and place on the sheet.
8. Cover with a towel and let rise in a warm place for 1 to 1-1/2 hours, or until nearly doubled in size.
9. Slash the tops of the loaves diagonally with a sharp razor blade.
10. Mist the loaves lightly with water and bake at 450°F (232°C) for 10 minutes.
11. Reduce the heat to 400°F (204°C) and continue baking for 35 minutes longer, or until the bread is done when tested.
12. For a harder crust, place a pan of hot water on the bottom of the oven and mist with water several times during baking. Remove the pan of water after 15 minutes of baking.
13. Turn out onto a wire rack and cool.
